name: Trivy
description: Comprehensive security scanner for containers, filesystems, git repos, and IaC
when_to_use: Scan Docker images before deploy, scan repos for vulnerabilities and misconfigs
what_it_finds: CVEs in OS packages and language deps, IaC misconfigs, exposed secrets, license issues

name: Semgrep
description: Static analysis tool finding bugs and security issues using pattern-matching rules
when_to_use: Code review, CI pipelines, security audits — supports custom rules
what_it_finds: SQL injection, XSS, insecure deserialization, hardcoded secrets, OWASP Top 10

name: Gitleaks
description: Scan git repos for hardcoded secrets like API keys, passwords, and tokens
when_to_use: Before commits (pre-commit hook), in CI, auditing existing repos
what_it_finds: API keys, passwords, tokens, private keys, cloud credentials in source code and git history

install.command: brew install gitleaks || go install github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/v8@latest

name: Snyk CLI
description: Find and fix vulnerabilities in dependencies, containers, and IaC
when_to_use: Dependency scanning in CI, container security, IaC review
what_it_finds: Vulnerable dependencies, container vulnerabilities, IaC misconfigurations

name: Grype
description: Vulnerability scanner for container images and filesystems — scans OS packages, language dependencies, and SBOMs against multiple CVE databases
when_to_use: Scanning container images and project directories for known vulnerabilities; pairs with Syft for SBOM generation
what_it_finds: CVEs in OS packages, language-specific dependencies, container image vulnerabilities with severity ratings

name: OSV-Scanner
description: Google's open-source vulnerability scanner — checks dependencies against the OSV database covering all major ecosystems
when_to_use: Scanning lockfiles and SBOMs for known vulnerabilities using Google's comprehensive OSV vulnerability database
what_it_finds: Known vulnerabilities in npm, PyPI, Go, Maven, Cargo, and other ecosystem dependencies

name: TruffleHog
description: Scans git repos, S3 buckets, and filesystems for leaked secrets — verifies found credentials are live by testing them against APIs
when_to_use: Deep secret scanning with verification, auditing git history for leaked credentials, scanning cloud storage
what_it_finds: API keys, passwords, tokens, cloud credentials — uniquely verifies if secrets are still active

name: OpenSSF Scorecard
description: Automated security assessment tool for open source projects — checks for branch protection, dependency updates, code review practices, and more
when_to_use: Evaluating the security posture of open source dependencies, improving your own project's security practices
what_it_finds: Missing branch protection, lack of code review, outdated dependencies, unsigned releases, dangerous workflow patterns

name: Bearer
description: Static application security testing (SAST) tool that finds security risks and vulnerabilities in source code with data flow analysis
when_to_use: Security scanning in CI pipelines, detecting data leaks, finding OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities with low false-positive rates
what_it_finds: SQL injection, XSS, sensitive data exposure, insecure configurations, data flow vulnerabilities

name: Syft
description: Software bill of materials (SBOM) generator — creates SPDX and CycloneDX SBOMs from container images, filesystems, and archives
when_to_use: Generating SBOMs for compliance, supply chain transparency, and feeding into vulnerability scanners like Grype
what_it_finds: Complete inventory of packages, libraries, and dependencies in containers and projects
